name: Apple Butter
recipeInstructions:
Yield: About 8 to 9 pints
Use Jonathan, Winesap, Stayman, Golden Delicious, McIntosh, or other tasty apple varieties for good results.
Procedure: Wash the apples, remove stems, and quarter and core them. Cook slowly in cider and vinegar until soft. Press the fruit through a colander, food mill, or strainer. Cook the fruit pulp with sugar and spices, stirring frequently. To test for doneness, remove a spoonful of the mixture and let it sit without steam for 2 minutes. It is done if the butter remains mounded on the spoon. Another way to determine when the butter is cooked adequately is to spoon a small quantity onto a plate. When a rim of liquid does not separate around the edge of the butter, it is ready for canning. Fill hot jars with the butter, leaving 1/4-inch headspace. Quart jars need not be presterilized. Adjust lids and process according to the recommendations in Table 1.
Table 1. Recommended processing time for Apple Butter in a boiling-water canner.
Style of Pack: Hot
Jar Size: Half-pints or Pints
Process Time at Altitudes of 0 - 1,000 ft: 5 minutes
Process Time at Altitudes of 1,001 - 6,000 ft: 10 minutes
Process Time at Altitudes of Above 6,000 ft: 15 minutes
Style of Pack: Hot
Jar Size: Quarts
Process Time at Altitudes of 0 - 1,000 ft: 10 minutes
Process Time at Altitudes of 1,001 - 6,000 ft: 15 minutes
Process Time at Altitudes of Above 6,000 ft: 20 minutes
